properties.title: Abbey Road Farm
properties.description: Abbey Road Farm is a unique destination with a luxurious converted silo inn (B&B), beautifully crafted winery, indoor/outdoor tasting room, and events venue located on a bucolic 82-acre working farm and vineyard in Oregon’s Yamhill-Carlton AVA . Their estate vineyard highlights 15 organically-farmed varietals grown on 45 gently sloping acres, which produce both Abbey Road Farm wine and the Wilkens Family Reserve label on-location with respect to old-world philosophies and a focus on modern techniques.

properties.title: Abbott Claim
properties.description: Located in the Yamhill-Carlton AVA of Oregon’s Willamette Valley, Abbott Claim is perched atop the Savannah Ridge, a 170-acre boundary that hosts some of the oldest soils in the Willamette Valley. As the next generation of stewards of this land, the team has implemented organic and regenerative farming practices in the vineyard and its surroundings since 2018, crafting examples of Pinot Noir and Chardonnay that truly speak to place.

properties.title: Adelsheim Vineyard
properties.description: Adelsheim, located in Newberg, Ore., was founded with an optimistic spirit and a lofty dream: to create world-class Pinot noir and Chardonnay in an undiscovered wine region, the Chehalem Mountains. Since 1971, we have obsessively pursued exceptional wines of place, that celebrate the unique bounty of our estate vineyards. As a founding winery of the Willamette Valley and Chehalem Mountains first winery, Adelsheim has played an instrumental role in nearly every aspect of the Oregon wine story. As leaders, we believe it is our duty to be steadfast stewards of this abundant land and to rally our community in support of equity and education. Adelsheim is a LIVE certified sustainable winery, and a seven-time Wine & Spirits Top 100 Winery. For more information, visit our website.

properties.title: Alexana Winery
properties.description: Our Gold LEED-Certified tasting room and 3 level gravity flow winery is the perfect place to explore the diversity and complexity of the Willamette Valley. With eighteen various sedimentary and volcanic soil types, our uniquely situated estate vineyard has earned a reputation for producing highly rated wines with exceptional balance and character. 'Diverse Soils, Complex Wines'

properties.title: Alloro Vineyard
properties.description: Alloro Vineyard is 110 acres of uniquely contoured, loess-derived, Laurelwood soil. Located on a southwest-facing slope in Oregon's Chehalem Mountains, this special place is the heart and soul of our wines and is personally cared for by founder David Nemarnik. We are a limited production producer of 100% Estate wines, crafting all our wines from our sustainably farmed single vineyard site, which also hosts a diverse mixture of many other crops, plants, and animals forming an extraordinary whole farm setting. Our UC Davis educated, Burgundy trained winemaker, Tom Fitzpatrick, believes that a wine should be an "expression of place, at a moment in time" and should therefore capture the unique personality of a site as it is expressed in each vintage. Terroir-driven wines that reflect this philosophy are our focus.

properties.title: Amalie Robert Estate
properties.description:
Amalie Robert Estate was founded at the turn of the century by Dena Drews and Ernie Pink. Dena and Ernie left the corporate world behind and began a journey to pursue a vision. What we found was a beautiful Willamette Valley cherry orchard, and the opportunity to take a hand in our own destiny.

"Bob, I think I got here too late. You have your cherry orchard on top of my vineyard!"

Ernie was pretty sure he saw his vineyard beneath that old orchard and struck a deal to find out. The cherries were harvested in the summer of 1999 and the transformation to vineyard began in earnest. With the help of some new friends, it was Earth Day 2000 when the last vine was planted, and the vineyard at Amalie Robert Estate was established.

properties.title: Amaterra Wines
properties.description:
Located just minutes from downtown Portland, Amaterra is perched high in the slopes of the city’s West Hills. As a member, you can sip, savor, and enjoy bounty from one of the most unique wine terroirs in the country. Our estate overlooks unparalleled views of the Willamette Valley, embracing the landscape of the Pacific Northwest. 

At Amaterra, our process allows for the gentlest handling of fruit while maintaining desired structure and delicate aromatics. We apply a unique winemaking process using bridge cranes that allow one elevation drop to act as a multiple-level winemaking facility. This allows us to control tannin extraction and preserve delicate aromatics. The design also enables our winemaking team to see all areas of the process and improve material handling efficiency. 

Amaterra also carries sister brand 51Weeks, created by our winemaker Matt Vuylsteke and his wife Nancy. 51Weeks Winemaking sources ripe fruit predominantly from the Columbia Valley, both in Washington and Oregon. These vineyards are in dry, sun-drenched locations and the soils are various series of silt loams.

properties.title: Ambar Estate
properties.description: Ambar Estate is the first Regenerative Organic Certified® vineyard in the Willamette Valley. Ambar is founded upon the belief that great wine is born from a thoughtful collaboration between science and nature, with a dose of creativity. We welcome guests for exceptional hospitality experiences, where they can learn about regenerative viticulture and winemaking and enjoy our wines in a beautiful and tranquil vineyard setting.

properties.title: Anacréon Winery
properties.description:
We named our winery Anacréon after the prolific Greek poet (582-485 BC). Known for his poems about love and wine, Anacréon captured the light and beauty of these timeless elements in an elegant way, one that has been revered by many poets and writers since. Like the poet, we see love and wine going hand-in-hand. Anacréon spoke to us as a perfect namesake for the special hillside we call home. 

Personally curated wine-tasting experiences at Anacréon Winery are unique and intimate. Join us at our organically farmed vineyard, Belle Colline, located at the home of Anacréon Winery owners Danell and Kipp Myers.
A true ‘boutique’ winery by definition, we produce less than 1000 cases per vintage. Our focus is on combining the magic of the terroir and microclimate of the Chehalem Mountains with the highest quality winemaking practices. Showcasing our beautiful wines in a relaxed and fun environment on our stunning property allows us to create something very personal and special for our guests.

Anacréon has two distinct and special wine clubs - "Poetic Justice" and the "Anacréontic Society." This is our own community of wine enthusiasts who love laughter, great conversations, and exquisite events with food and wine. 

Our Belle Colline Vineyard is an ORGANICALLY CERTIFIED vineyard on a well-regarded stretch in the Chehalem Mountains AVA.

By visiting our website, you can join our email list to receive special offers about our upcoming events and offers.

properties.title: Andante Vineyard
properties.description:
Wine flights and food pairings are offered in our beautiful new tasting room at our family owned, LIVE Certified, Salmon Safe vineyard in the Van Duzer Corridor AVA. 

Through organic and sustainable management practices, we ensure that our vines develop healthy, happy root systems and balanced canopies. These practices help us to guide and create rich complexity within our wines. We wholeheartedly believe that the best wines start in the vineyard. Our winemaking approach is to guide and trust in these natural processes to best express the character of the vineyard.

Sauvignon blanc, Chardonnay, Aligoté, Pinot noir, Rosé of Pinot noir and White Pinot noir are among the varietals that we offer.

properties.title: Anderson Family Vineyard
properties.description: Steep Slopes. Deep Roots. Intense Flavors.Our rocky terraces produce grapes for Oregons most esteemed wineries. Now, very limited handmade bottlings are available at direct purchase pricing. Pinot noir and Dijon Chardonnay. Drop dead beautiful site. Dundee Hills off Red Hills Road.

properties.title: Antica Terra
properties.description:
Winemaker Maggie Harrison produces rare bottlings from the Willamette Valley and Santa Barbara under the labels Antica Terra, Lillian, and the Glories.

Join us in the glowing enclave of our newly-expanded Barrel Hall for a paired tasting, a lunch, or a flight of rarities from our cellar. Each experience delves into the wine we make, the producers from around the world that we love to collect, and soigné culinary additions from the hands of chef, Timothy Wastell.

In the high season, let us take you into the acres of native oak woodland crowning our vineyard property where you will enjoy a beautifully-appointed picnic and hosted flight at our 200-foot-long Table in the Trees. 

Book a tasting on our site, or let us build an itinerary of the top experiences in the Willamette Valley with you. 

Email meet@anticaterra.com or visit our website for more.

properties.title: Apolloni Vineyards
properties.description:
Apolloni Vineyards, a family-owned and operated estate property in the Tualatin Hills AVA in the Northern Willamette Valley, showcasing estate grown and organically farmed cool climate varietals; Pinot Noir, Chardonnay, Pinot Gris, and Pinot Blanc. We are known for premium Pinot Noir and Italian style wines. Our winery and vineyard are certified sustainable by the LIVE and Salmon Safe programs. 

The Apolloni winemaking tradition dates back over 150 years, with family vineyards in northern Italy. When Alfredo started his own family, he and his wife Laurine knew they wanted to raise their children in the shadow of the vines. They searched for just the right location, one which would nurture the Pinot Noir grapes, but also allow for the exploration of cooler climate varietals, including the stainless white wines of Alfredo’s family tradition. In 1999, they moved to Oregon, established their vineyard on a piece of land in the northernmost reaches of the Willamette Valley, tucked up against the Coast Range and protected by forest and a natural rain shadow. 

Check our website for more information and follow us @apolloniwine on Facebook and Instagram. Just a quick jaunt off Highway 6 to Tillamook (and 25 miles west of Portland), it is an easy stop to pick up wine for your weekend at the beach.

properties.title: Appassionata Estate
properties.description:
Appassionata Estate began as a passion project for the renowned German winemaker Ernst Loosen. Erni’s lifelong love for exploring the world’s great Pinot Noirs drew him to Oregon nearly three decades ago. Over many visits, collecting wines and friends along the way, he realized that something special was going on in the Willamette Valley and began to dream of making wine here himself.

Never one to merely dream, Erni launched a small, collaborative project with J. Christopher Wines in 2005, bottling a very limited, highly selected Pinot Noir that he named Appassionata. A few vintages later, he put down roots in Oregon, purchasing 40 acres in the Chehalem Mountains, planting the Appassionata Vineyard, and building the winery. His long-term vision was to establish an enduring family wine estate, dedicated to growing and producing exquisitely expressive wines with extraordinary ageability. It has been two decades in the making, but that vision is now being fully realized.

properties.title: Arabilis
properties.description: We are Allison & Kenny McMahon. Our name, ‘Arabilis,’ meaning arable or farmable land, embodies our close partnership with farmers and our aspiration to own a vineyard someday. A husband-wife team with two PhDs in wine, we combine scientific expertise with meticulous vineyard sourcing to craft acclaimed Chardonnay, Pinot Noir, and traditional method sparkling wines. Each sip of our wines embodies our journey of dedication, love, and relentless hustle.

properties.title: Archery Summit
properties.description: Since its inception in 1993, Archery Summit has been devoted to crafting exceptional Pinot Noir from its more than 80 acres across five estate vineyards. Our philosophy is driven by a commitment to understanding the intricacies and unique personalities of each of our estate vineyards, allowing us to craft wines that vividly express their distinct places of origin. Over the past twenty-five years we have developed an international reputation for producing wines among the finest and most distinct of New World Pinot Noir. Our tasting room is open daily from 10am to 4pm by appointment. For more information on tastings and to reserve an experience, visit https://www.archerysummit.com/visit/ . We look forward to hosting you during your visit.

properties.title: Argyle Winery
properties.description: A pioneer in the Oregon wine industry, Argyle was founded in 1987 by Rollin Soles to capture the unique terroir of the Willamette Valley in a sparkling wine. Now Argyle produces one of the most diverse portfolios in Oregon Pinot Noir, Chardonnay, Riesling and Sparkling wines. Visit us in downtown Dundee, Oregon the heart of the Willamette Valley. Enjoy seated tastings in our stunning Tasting House, or on our outdoor porch or patio. You are also welcome to purchase wine (including chilled sparkling and whites) to go.

properties.title: Arlyn Vineyard
properties.description: It is an honor to welcome new friends to the farm and share the journey of this project. Whether you want to meet the farm animals and puppies, walk through the vines, immersed in farming practices or focus specifically on what is being poured in those pretty Zaltos, we look forward to greeting you. Owner Janis Pate hosts seated tastings by appointment.

properties.title: Artist Block Wine
properties.description:
Artist Block tasting room in the Willamette Valley lies in the prestigious Dundee Hills, surrounded by world-famous wineries.

Artist Block exists to create fine wine of integrity, made with creativity for a new generation of wine drinkers, and to blur the boundaries between the worlds of wine and art. Sourcing from the highest quality organically farmed sites in the Willamette Valley, our wines are curated by a female Master of Wine with the conscious consumer in mind. 

Artist Block aims to create experiences that connect our guests to the diverse beauty and complexity of our people and place in this great region.

properties.title: Aubaine
properties.description:
Aubaine is a high-end boutique project of single vineyard and estate only Chardonnay and Pinot noir. Pronounced “Ō-Bane,” Aubaine is the French term for “Windfall.” The green wave on the label represents the Eola-Amity Hills, and the blue wave represents the winds of the Van Duzer Corridor. Our inaugural release came from the 2019 vintage, crafted from our beautiful Anahata Vineyard.

We currently have two separate estate vineyards. Our first is Anahata Vineyard which surrounds our high end luxury retreat, AtTheJoy. The name of the vineyard is the Sanskrit word for the heart chakra, with blocks named after translations of the word JOY. Planted in 2015 to various clones of Pinot Noir and Chardonnay, Anahata is the first expression of our single-vineyard estate wine project.

Our second legacy property is the Windfall Vineyard, a 117-acre masterpiece on the west side of the Eola-Amity Hills, directly facing the Van Duzer Corridor. The steady winds funneled through this gap create the perfect environment for its southwest-facing slopes, yielding Pinot Noir and Chardonnay of remarkable purity. The long, warm days and cool nights at Windfall provide the balance and structure that will define world-class wines for years to come. Fruit from Windfall was first harvested in 2024 and will debut in Aubaine’s upcoming releases.

properties.title: August Cellars
properties.description: August Cellars, located at the crest of Rex Hill, is a wonderful stop! We produce crisp, clean white wines, Pinot noir, and Marechal Foch. Our wines are appropriate for all wine drinkers. August Cellars is also the home of several small boutique wineries who share the facility. For a complete listing of wines for sale and other wineries visit our website. We do not require reservations.  Please just walk on in! Friday-Sunday 11-5.

properties.title: Aurora Vineyards
properties.description:
A gem in the Willamette Valley, Aurora Vineyards was established in 2002. Our Tasting Room is situated perfectly on our lovely estate and boasts a large outdoor patio with waterfalls, pond, strolling paths, fire pit, and lawn for yard games during the summer months. Our on-site bistro offers an excellent menu of small plates to enjoy with your wine or tasting flights.

Our vineyards are located in the scenic Cascade Foothills of the Willamette Valley and include 34 acres of Pinot Noir, Chardonnay, Sauvignon Blanc, Pinot Blanc, Pinot Meunier, Gamay Noir, and Muscat.

properties.title: Barking Dog
properties.description:
Retired US Navy veterans planted their 15-acre vineyard in the high-elevation Chehalem Mountains AVA with a commitment to sustainability and community. Partnering with winemaker Jim Sanders, they produce single-vineyard Pinot Noirs that reflect the land's character. Their veteran-owned vineyard, home to a pack of rescued Golden Retrievers, thrives with biodiversity initiatives like the Bee Pollinator Pathway. Every bottle mirrors their dedication to stewardship, craftsmanship, and giving back - one sip at a time. 
Visit our tasting room in downtown Carlton to experience our wines firsthand. Open Friday and Saturday or by appointment, we invite you to explore our cozy space, enjoy a flight, and take in our rotating art exhibits. 
Check out our upcoming special events - whether at the tasting room or on the road, we might be pouring near you! Follow us on social media or visit our website to stay up to date.

properties.title: Battle Creek by Browne Family
properties.description:
We’re Battle Creek Cellars (and we’re Oregon Pinot Noir)
At Battle Creek, we embrace Oregon’s pioneering spirit with a blend of rugged elegance and down-to-earth sensibility. Our wines reflect a simple, approachable style, driven by dedicated farming, thoughtful winemaking, and a deep respect for the land. With a genuine love for our region, we are proud to earn a place at your table. Focused on creating artisan wines without the fuss, we craft each bottle with care and authenticity. Whether from our namesake Battle Creek Vineyard or other cherished vineyards across the state, our wines are a true reflection of Oregon and the Willamette Valley.
Beyond our wines, Battle Creek is more than just a place to taste—it's a home away from home, a third space where our community gathers, connects, and experiences the warmth of authentic hospitality. We invite you to be a part of this shared space, where genuine connections are made and celebrated.

properties.title: Beaux Frères
properties.description: As guests arrive to Beaux Frres, it becomes immediately evident that this place is first and foremost a farma family of pigs run along the side of the driveway, a converted barn houses our thirty-year old winery, a firetruck used for water distribution sits amidst a swath of old Oregon oak trees, and a handful of winery dogs lay, rain or shine, on the crush pad to greet you.Much of the farm remains the same from when it was founded by Michael Etzel and his brother-in-law, Robert Parker, back in 1988, with their first vintage released in 1992. Our approach to farming our 26-acre Beaux Frres vineyard and 9-acre Upper Terrace vineyard is guided by holistic, sustainable principles and we aim to make wines that are based on minimal intervention and a shared intuition with our land.

properties.title: Belle Pente Vineyard & Winery
properties.description: Belle Pente (bell-pont) means “beautiful slope”- a perfect description for our south facing hillside overlooking the misty shrouds of Oregon"s Coast Range mountains. Our 23 acres of sustainably farmed vineyards are planted with the traditional grapes of Burgundy & Alsace. Our limited-production wines are made gently and naturally to highlight varietal fruit flavors and the unique characteristics of each vineyard site.

properties.title: Bellingar Estates
properties.description: Bellingar Estates is a brand-new, family owned and operated vineyard just outside of Newberg. Kim and Evan Bellingar have spent their entire careers in the wine industry right here in the Willamette Valley, and bring the highlights of their experience to you through their beautiful array of Pinot noir, Chardonnay, Syrah and Red Blend wines. The craft-filled tasting room experience is a true expression of the Oregon spirit, with delightfully crafted wines, and a fun and jovial atmosphere. Join the dozens of people who have recently discovered this new hidden gem and join us at Bellingar Estates!

properties.title: Benton-Lane Winery
properties.description: Benton-Lane was founded in 1988, a pioneer of the then-uncharted Willamette Valley. Nestled in a coastal mountain range with long sunny days and cool evenings, Benton-Lane is a 317-acre estate cloaked by thick forest and miles of pristine wilderness. The 140-acre vineyard, with a mix of sedimentary and volcanic soils that allow for complex terroir expression, produces world-class Pinot Noir and Chardonnay balanced between freshness and intensity.

properties.title: Benza Vineyards
properties.description:
Benza Vineyards, founded by Ron Benza and Trish Damkroger, presents a sanctuary for wine enthusiasts to experience the elegant, complex flavors of the region meticulously crafted by winemaker Paden West. Nestled amidst tranquil surroundings with picturesque vineyard views and an idyllic farm setting with wraparound porches, guests are invited to indulge in Benza’s wines from the Laurelwood soil while enjoying the company of friendly dogs and chickens, and personalized hospitality, making it the perfect day escape just 25 minutes from Portland.


CCOF Organic Certified
LIVE Certified Sustainable Grapes
Salmon Safe

CROCKETT RODNEY M & CAROL L REV LIV TRUST
BENZA RONALD J & DAMKROGER PATRICIA A
BENZA RONALD J & DAMKROGER PATRICIA A
BENZA RONALD J & DAMKROGER PATRICIA A

properties.title: Bergström Wines
properties.description:
Since 1999, my family and team have been working towards our vision of stewarding naturally and responsibly farmed, estate vineyard terroirs that best represent the diverse appellations of our world-class Willamette Valley. Our Pinot Noirs and Chardonnays are a three-dimensional vinous tour of our beautiful state’s ancient volcanic and marine sedimentary hillsides.

All of us here at Bergström Wines are humbled by, and thankful for, the unwavering support of our community and clients. We give thanks to those who continue to make our labor of love possible by supporting our wines.

All tastings and wine pickups are held at our family home in the Dundee Hills. For information regarding reservations please email us at reservations@bergstromwines.com

Thank you once again for your support of our wines and our family and the Oregon winegrowing community!

Josh Bergström

properties.title: Bethel Heights Vineyard
properties.description:
Bethel Heights Vineyard is a multi-generational family estate established by the Casteel family in 1977 in the Eola-Amity Hills of Oregon’s Willamette Valley.  Ben Casteel became Bethel Heights’ second generation winemaker in 2006. Over the years the Estate has grown to eighty acres of mature vines, all organically farmed, now including the adjacent Justice Vineyard and Lewman Vineyard.
Our geologically complex hillside soils, directly impacted by the powerful Aeolian winds that define the Eola-Amity Hills, yield highly energized wines with firm backbones, depth of character, and distinctive personalities. Sixteen different bottlings of estate grown Pinot noir and Chardonnay each year reflect the evolving narrative of our three estate vineyards.

properties.title: Big Table Farm
properties.description: Big Table Farm is the collaborative effort of Brian Marcy and Clare Carver. A winemaker and an artist, we grow and produce what we love to eat and drink. We have a working farm and host tastings by appointment at our atelier location in Carlton. We produce Chardonnay, Pinot Gris, Pinot Noir and Syrah.

properties.title: Björnson Vineyard
properties.description:
Welcome to Björnson Vineyard, where every moment offers Insta-worthy views, laid-back vibes, and seriously good wine. Nestled in the heart of Oregon's picturesque Willamette Valley, our vineyard boasts breathtaking scenery that rivals the taste of our exceptional Pinot Noir.

Speaking of wine, we specialize in Pinot Noir, Chardonnay, and Gamay Noir, but our latest obsessions include Gouais Blanc (we are the only commercial producers in the Western Hemisphere!), along with Aligoté, Sauvignon Blanc, and more, all of which are making waves in the wine world.

But here's what sets us apart: we're not just about the wine. We're also committed eco-warriors, employing sustainable farming practices and renewable energy solutions. Because, let's face it, saving the planet is pretty cool, right?

So, hit the road and let's sip some wine together. We'll provide you with the best views and even better vibes. We can't wait to welcome you to Björnson Vineyard—see you soon!

Note - while we are dog-friendly during our outdoor seating (no dogs inside0, we ask that dogs remain on leash at all times.

properties.title: Bledsoe|McDaniels
properties.description:
Bledsoe McDaniels Winery is the payoff for our willingness to embrace risk and go
deep on fourth and short. Emerging from the Bledsoe Family Winery “idea laboratory” and in response to consumer demand, Bledsoe McDaniels Winery is the stunning outcome
of Drew Bledsoe and Josh McDaniels’ long-standing desire to make their favorite wine of the Willamette Valley – Pinot Noir – along with homegrown Syrah. Like all Bledsoe wines, these are balanced, elegant and complex wines that demonstrate a unique sense of place
while showcasing both the differences and similarities between the renowned valleys.

properties.title: Bluebird Hill Cellars
properties.description: ​Our wines are small-lot and handcrafted, and always will be, with a commitment to make distinctive wines from exceptional fruit using a combination of traditional and modern winemaking techniques with a focus on detail. As our vineyard has matured, we are now making the majority of our wines from estate fruit, along with fruit purchased from select vineyards in the Willamette Valley and other Pacific Northwest regions. Our Pinot noirs and Chardonnays have received an array of awards and excellent scores in wine judgings and festivals. We also produce Pinot gris, Rosé, Syrah, a Syrah/Pinot Red Blend, and dessert wines.

properties.title: Boedecker Cellars
properties.description:
Nestled in Portland’s NW Industrial neighborhood, Boedecker Cellars has been crafting exceptional wines since 2003. Known as Portland’s premium Pinot Noir house, we focus on creating sustainable, age-worthy wines that embody the essence of the Willamette Valley.
Our welcoming urban winery and spacious tasting room include a cozy lounge area, perfect for relaxing while enjoying a thoughtfully curated flight of five wines, savoring a glass, or sharing a bottle with friends.  Cheese and charcuterie boards are also available to complement your tasting experience.
Join us Thursday through Sunday afternoons. Reservations are encouraged but not required and walk-ins are welcome.
Discover the perfect pairing of craftsmanship, hospitality, and flavors at Boedecker Cellars. We look forward to hosting you soon!

properties.title: Brick House Wine Co.
properties.description: We offer wines distinguished by both the elegance and the simplicity of the old ways. All Brick House wines are grown on our forty acre farm; the product of low-yielding vines handled with great care. The farm was certified organic in 1990 and Biodynamic by Demeter in 2005. Our vineyards are planted to the three traditional varieties of Burgundy: Pinot Noir, Chardonnay and Gamay Noir. We strive to live and work in harmony with nature.

properties.title: Brigadoon Wine Co
properties.description:
Nestled against the coastal foothills of the Southern Willamette Valley, just northwest of Eugene, Brigadoon Wine Company represents a multi-generational commitment to sustainable agriculture and organic viticulture. From soil prep to planting, each decision made is done so with the ultimate goal of producing truly great wines, wines that speak of place.

Our 66 acre organically farmed vineyard straddles an unnamed tributary of Ferguson Creek, and is home to warm days, cool nights and soils necessary to create world class wines. Our soils, classified as “Bellpine” by the USGS, have a layer of sand stone below the surface, forcing the roots to dive deeper during the late stages of the growing season to scavenge for nutrients and water.

Our vineyard site is generally cooler than our neighbors, typically one week behind theirs. The additional time with fruit on the vine allows us the opportunity to achieve optimal physiological and flavor development, without producing high sugars and resulting in high alcohol wines.

properties.title: Brittan Vineyards
properties.description:
It took years to find the ideal site on which to grow Pinot Noir but after an exhaustive search, Brittan Vineyards began on an 128-acre hillside in Oregon in December 2004. The soils are a mixture of glacial deposits and volcanic material, with a predominant presence of broken basalt.

At that time, there were 18 acres of existing vines. However, close to 50% has subsequently been re-planted in order to fine-tune the viticultural practices and improve the clonal and rootstock combinations. This included one of the first plantings of the Swan clonal selection of Pinot Noir in Oregon.

After dropping all of the crop in 2005 to allow the vines to get better established, the first two Pinot Noirs from the mature vines on the property came from the 2006 vintage, a total of 720 cases.

In the spring of 2008, an additional 3.5 acres of Pinot Noir were planted, which are now in production— the rocky soils and low water retention often means that it takes five years for a vine to get into production.

In addition to the Pinot, 4 acres of Chardonnay and 1.5 acres of Syrah have been planted. These are two varietals that benefit greatly from a cool growing climate.

properties.title: Broadley Vineyards Winery
properties.description:
Welcome to Broadley Vineyards Winery! Located conveniently halfway between Corvallis and Eugene, Oregon, on Highway 99W along the scenic Long Tom River, our winery offers a memorable wine tasting experience. Since our inaugural vintage in 1986, we've been driven by a passion for crafting exceptional wines that reflect the unique character of our sustainably tended vineyards.

Wine Tasting Near Corvallis and Eugene, Oregon:

At Broadley Vineyards Winery, you can taste our exquisite wines and explore our dynamic collection. We feature hand-crafted Pinot Noirs, Gamay Noir, Chardonnay, and Pinot Gris, each with bold, rich, layered flavors that stand the test of time. Our 33-acre estate vineyard, dry-farmed and organically nurtured, embodies our commitment to sustainable practices for a thriving future.

Discover more about our dedication to crafting outstanding wines and promoting sustainable living by visiting us. Experience the best wine tasting near Corvallis and Eugene, Oregon, and let our passion for wine inspire you.

properties.title: Brooks
properties.description:
Celebrating over 20 years of sustainable winegrowing and winemaking, focusing on Riesling and Pinot Noir. We welcome you to our estate winery where we offer a variety of experiences from our daily flights, to seated tastings, to estate tours. Our executive chef features exquisite pairings and a variety of dishes to enjoy. 
Founded in 1998 by Jimi Brooks, a passionate leader for organic and biodynamic farming. While Jimi is no longer with us, his dynamic spirit lives on through his wines, family and friends. With the outpouring of generosity by the Oregon wine community, Brooks Wines continues to produce beautiful Riesling and Pinot noir!
Brooks is the only winery in the world that is a certified B Corporation, member of 1% For the Planet and Demeter Biodynamic certified.

properties.title: Bryn Mawr Vineyards
properties.description: Bryn Mawr Vineyards was purchased by Jon and Kathy Lauer as a tiny garagiste winery in 2009. With a 4 acre foundation of established Pinot Noir, Chardonnay, and Tempranillo vines, they partnered with winemaker Rachel Rose to diversify the site, adding Pinot Blanc, Dolcetto, and Riesling vines among many new clones of the aforementioned standards. Wine was made between Jon's basement and a former wedding tent for 8 vintages. In 2018, our new winery facility was completed, followed by a new tasting room in 2019. The last decade has brought us national recognition for our Pinot Noir and Chardonnay programs, but it's our playfully experimental side that has made us beloved locally. We act as an important bridge, honoring the wines that make our valley world-renowned while pushing the envelope for what's possible.Perched at 870 ft. on the upper crest of the acclaimed Eola-Amity Hills, our sustainable vineyard is often battered by powerful wind sweeping through the Van Duzer corridor from the Pacific Coast. Our vines are anchored into rocky, steep, and fast-draining volcanic soils, testing the upper limit of Willamette Valley viticulture. This unforgiving exposure produces powerful characters across our wines. Our whites and pinks are nervy, acid-driven, and refreshingly crisp even in the hottest of vintages, while our reds are bold, edgy, and dense. It also gives us one of the most dramatic views in the valley, with the Cascades, the Coast Range, and endless rolling hills as far as the eye can see.We offer multiple experiences, from casual glass and bottle service in our courtyard to private reserve tastings.

properties.title: Cana’s Feast Winery
properties.description: Cana's Feast Winery crafts bold, Northwest wines of richness and complexity that pair adeptly with food while expressing their varietal characteristics with depth and integrity. Known widely for their diverse selection of unique wines, Cana's Feast has made a niche for itself by producing varietally expressive, site-specific wines grown from some of the Pacific Northwest region's most highly-regarded farmer/grower-owned vineyards. Guests enjoy spectacular views of the Coastal Range while surrounded by the scent of olive trees, stone pines, and Italian cypress. Bocce courts beckon to those who want to try their hand at this ancient Roman game. The Tasting Room is open daily from 11am 5pm.

properties.title: Carlton Winemakers Studio
properties.description:
The Carlton Winemakers Studio has been the launching pad of some of Oregon’s most exciting producers for 20 years. A winery collective, tasting room and wine shop all in one, The Studio is here to help you explore the Willamette Valley in one central location.
 
While over a dozen small producers are crafting their wares by utilizing the shared winery space and equipment, our Tasting Room showcases their wines in a series of rotating flights that change weekly. With our winemakers sourcing fruit from every AVA in the Willamette Valley, as well as Southern Oregon and Washington, you’ll be able to experience the artistry of fermentation through each of their unique perspectives.
 
Call ahead to see who we are featuring today or just pop on in to be surprised. If you’re lucky you might even find one of our winemakers pouring behind the bar.

Cheese & Charcuterie available via a temperature controlled vending machine that features curated selections of artisanal meats, cheeses, crackers, nuts and many local items.

properties.title: Chehalem Winery
properties.description:
Welcome to Chehalem, where our story is rooted in a deep and abiding reverence for the land. Located in the heart of Oregon’s Willamette Valley, we aim to reflect what the vineyard gives us with minimal processing and without compromise. At Chehalem Estate Tasting Room, you are invited to taste our wines with a view of the land where they were grown.

Our Estate Tasting Room Experience is the perfect introduction to our wines and story. For Club members or longtime fans of Chehalem wines, deepen your knowledge and taste through some of Chehalem’s most spectacular vintages with an intimate, guided tasting in our Library Loft.

We’re open daily from 10:30 a.m. to 5:30 p.m. 

We will be closed Christmas Day and New Year’s Day.
We will be closing at 3 pm on Christmas Eve and New Year’s Eve.

Reservations are available for your convenience, and walk-ins are always welcome. For assistance with booking or questions about our experiences, please contact our dedicated concierge service at (503) 864-3404. 

Toasting you all in good health and spirits,
Chehalem Winery Team

properties.title: Chemeketa Cellars
properties.description:
A visit to Chemeketa Cellars supports Chemeketa Community College's Wine Studies program. Each sip of wine represents our students' hands-on training from grapes to glass. The Chemeketa Eola campus includes an 8-acre teaching vineyard and a fully functioning estate winery on premise, sitting above the banks of the Willamette River in West Salem. Using our vineyard’s world-class fruit to create award-winning wines, students and faculty exchange knowledge to craft a variety of wines that fully express the terroir of our gathering place at Chemeketa Eola. Each vintage of Chemeketa Cellars wine provides a lesson for our vineyard and winemaking students and experimentation is encouraged and stewarded by our professional faculty. Students in the Wine Studies program go on to find employment in vineyards, wineries, and tasting rooms throughout the Willamette Valley and beyond.

Visit the Chemeketa Cellars tasting room, where we host community events like wine appreciation and paint-and-sip classes, live music, and an annual barrel tasting event featuring our alumni wineries. At Chemeketa Cellars, you can learn more about the program and the college, experience a unique wine tasting, and feel good knowing your purchase supports our students and program.

Visit our website for current tasting room hours and a calendar of events: www.chemeketacellars.com,

properties.title: CHO Wines
properties.description:
— CHO Wines at Cho Vineyards —
CHO Wines was founded in 2020 by Dave and Lois Cho with the desire to craft authentic wines with distinct character from higher elevation sites in Willamette Valley, Oregon. 

— The Wine —
CHO Wines has taken the Oregon wine industry by storm, having received 90+ scores by Wine Enthusiast, Wine Spectator and James Suckling. They were nominated by USA Today's Readers Choice 10 Best New Winery Category and their sparkling wines made The Enthusiast 100 List in 2022 and 2024, blind tasted amongst 23,000+ world renowned wines.

— The Vineyard —
The Chos, Oregon's first Korean-American winemakers, are developing 77 acres in the Willamette Valley into a sustainable vineyard, Oregon white oak savanna, and projected winery and tasting room build for March 2024. They planted 8 acres of Pinot Noir, Chardonnay and Pinot Gris over the last year and plan to expand with lesser planted varieties. Located on Bald Peak at 650-1150 elevation, southwest facing slope on premium volcanic and marine sedimentary soil characteristic of Chehalem Mountains and Ribbon Ridge AVAs.

properties.title: Chris James Cellars
properties.description:
Chris James Cellars is a family owned and operated vineyard and winery that produces unique and interesting wines in Carlton, Oregon. The vineyard was first planted in 2012, with additional blocks planted in each year since. We now have 25 acres planted with 16 different varietals. In 2015 we produced our first lots of Pinot Noir and Sauvignon Blanc and have since expanded our wine selection to offer hand-crafted lots of Pinot Noir, Sauvignon Blanc, Gewurztraminer, Riesling, Dolcetto, Lagrein, Barbera, Cab Franc and more.

Our approach to white wines is to emphasize and preserve the varietal aromatics, flavor and acidity. In red wines we seek to develop intense flavors through harvest decisions and winemaking techniques during fermentation and focus on mouthfeel and silky texture during maturation.

We now have two amazing tasting rooms to visit. The Estate Tasting Room is open Friday through Sunday, 12-5pm (April to November) and by appointment year round. It is located at our vineyard - 12000 NW Old Wagon Rd., Carlton, OR 97111. 

The McMinnville Tasting Room is open year round Sunday-Thursday 12-6pm, and Friday/Saturday 12-8pm. It is located at 645 NE 3rd St., McMinnville, OR 97128.

properties.title: Coeur de Terre Vineyard
properties.description: Coeur de Terre or "Heart of the Earth" is located in the West Hills of McMinnville, known for its geologic diversity and beauty which translates into unique Pinot Noir. Our vineyard has been farmed organicly since inception as we focus on small production, handcrafted, all estate Pinot Noir, Chardonnay, cool climate Syrah, Pinot Gris, Rosé, Riesling, and Grüner Veltliner. We would love to host you at our award-winning vineyard and winery!

properties.title: Coleman Vineyard
properties.description: Devoted to expressing the beauty, soil, and maritime-influenced climate of our vineyards in the Coast Range foothills of the Willamette Valley, our critically acclaimed Pinot noir and Pinot gris is estate grown and produced by the Coleman family. Come to expand your knowledge, enjoy the beautiful views, and savor the pleasures of a small wine estate.

properties.title: Colene Clemens Vineyards
properties.description:
— Colene Clemens Vineyards —
Colene Clemens was founded in 2005 by Joe and Victoria Stark with the express purpose of making exceptional Pinot noir. The property is named in honor of Victoria’s mother, Colene Clemens. Wine production began in 2008, and Winemaker & Vineyard Manager, Stephen Goff has been dedicated to the development and planting of the vineyard as well as the production of our stunning wines since the very beginning.

— The Estate —
Spanning 122 acres where the Chehalem Mountains converge with Ribbon Ridge, the estate contains both sedimentary soils from the Wellsdale & Hazelaire series, and volcanic soils from the Witzel series. Elevation climbs from 350 to 650 feet, where the winery perches with sweeping views of the coast range and valley. Currently 62 acres are planted to Pinot noir & Chardonnay.

— Visiting the Winery —
Our winery and patio are open for tasting by appointment. It's easy to plan your visit! To make your appointment, you can book online at www.coleneclemens.com/visit, give us a call at 503-662-4687, or email us at info@coleneclemens.com to secure your reservation prior to visiting. We look forward to hosting you!

properties.title: Compris Vineyard
properties.description:
At Compris Vineyard, we believe wine has the power to bring people together and create lasting memories. Nestled in the rolling hills of Newberg, Oregon, our 12.5-acre, organically farmed vineyard thrives in the Chehalem Mountains AVA, where diverse soils and a unique climate allow us to grow an array of grape varietals.

As a small, owner-operated winery, we take pride in crafting world-class wines with a hands-on approach, ensuring every bottle reflects our passion and dedication. Whether you’re a seasoned enthusiast or new to wine, we welcome you to experience the intimacy and warmth of our vineyard.

Join us to enjoy wines that capture the essence of our terroir, and our commitment to sustainability—connecting you not just to the wine but to the people and place behind it.

properties.title: Cooper Mountain Vineyards
properties.description: In 1987, Cooper Mountain Vineyards began a quest that has brought us to more than 100 acres, a commitment to qualitywinesand a welcomingtasting room. Today Cooper Mountain offers certified organic and biodynamic Pinot noir, Pinot gris, and Chardonnay wines. We invite you to come and be a part of our story.

properties.title: Corollary Wines
properties.description:
We founded Corollary Wines with a singular mission: to explore the terroir of Oregon's Willamette Valley and its expression in sparkling wine.

We ONLY make traditional-method sparkling wine. We believe that the Willamette Valley has all the right ingredients for world-class bubbles: diverse soils, cool and marginal microclimates, and a culture of thoughtful, sustainable farming.

properties.title: Cristom Vineyards
properties.description:
For over 30 years, Cristom Vineyards has crafted distinguished wines of place using traditional techniques—low yields, whole-cluster, and native yeast fermentations. Established in 1992 by the Gerrie family and now steered by the second generation, we steward five world-class vineyards on a windswept, east-facing volcanic slope in Oregon's Eola-Amity Hills. Our 240-acre Estate is farmed using biodynamic and organic methods to reveal the purest expression of terroir — resulting in honest, elegant, structured, and dynamic wines.

We welcome you to visit the Estate to discover expressive Pinot Noir and Chardonnay rooted in volcanic soils and crafted with intention. Choose a seated tasting with sweeping views or take an immersive vineyard tour—ride through our hillside vineyards, meet our flock of sheep, explore ancient basalt soils, and see how our holistic farming practices shape the wines we craft. Each visit offers a deeper connection to our land, our wines, and our 100-year vision for stewardship and craftsmanship.

properties.title: Crowley Wines
properties.description: We make our wines in the tradition of Burgundy and our Oregon forefathers. To us that means minimal intervention, yet maximum attention to detail. We feel that wines are made in the vineyard and our experience working in vineyards helps us understand that even when you prune, shoot thin and leaf-pull a vineyard to perfection, Mother Nature may still have her way with you and the grapes. Thus every vintage is wonderfully different and unique. We like it that way. We hope you do too.

properties.title: Cória Estates
properties.description:
Cória Estates is a Mexican-American Family-Owned Vineyard, Winery, and Tasting Room in the rolling hills of South Salem, Oregon. A distinctive wine experience that celebrates Mexican-American culture amidst the picturesque rolling hills of the Mid Willamette Valley. The estate has been growing grapes for 25 years in the Heart of the Willamette Valley.

Familia is at the heart of Cória Estates, a family-owned business that embodies the spirit of optimism and hard work bridging cultures through a Mexican-American and female-owned identity. Woven with the bond between a mother and daughter duo and a hard-working father/grower, our family produces exquisite fruit that forms the essence of our wines with minimal intervention. Our story of six decades of farming expertise with modern yet holistic winemaking ensures the fruit's natural beauty shines through with every sip.

As a bicultural family, the story of Luis and Janice begins from their separate family heritage and cultural backgrounds. Luis was born and raised in Mexico. He spent his early years helping his father growing vegetables and tending to the family livestock. He spent several years working near their family home, and by the time he was 14, ventured further seeking better opportunities and eventually found himself living in Oregon. Janice grew up in northern Idaho and had a normal small town upbringing. In the spring of 1979, while visiting family in Oregon, Janice met Luis. 

By 1981 they were married and by 1985 had welcomed two baby girls to the family! Life sped by, but Luis still had a need to get back to his roots of farming. In 1988 they purchased their first piece of property, a small 9 acre parcel which became the family home and later the original site of the vineyard. Early in 1997, the neighboring property became available, and they decided to take the chance and put down more roots into the mid-Willamette Valley. Early in the winter of 1999, they planted their first Pinot noir and Pinot gris vines with the help of family and friends. 

Now 18 years later, the vineyard is a mature 94 aces with Pinot noir, Pinot gris, and Chardonnay planted on mostly Nekia clay soils with elevations ranging from 400 to 850 feet. The whole family continues to be involved in what makes Coria Estates an exciting and thriving vineyard and winery. Luis and Janice continue to manage the vineyard and the administrative side of the business. Their youngest daughter Aurora is the winemaker, and one of only a few female winemakers in the state of Oregon. Their eldest daughter Elizabeth helps with tasting room events on the side of her professional job as a Speech Language Pathologist. Elizabeth’s husband Ty handles a variety of design & marketing tasks. 

All our wines are estate grown, and are now processed and poured on-site in our newly constructed production facility and tasting room, completed in June 2017. Our appreciation for our guests is top priority, and we make every effort to ensure your visit with us is hospitable, fun, and relaxed. Salud!

properties.title: David Paige Wines
properties.description: David Paige Wines was started in 2018, when Dave met a few like-minded enthusiasts and they bought a vineyard together in the Eola-Amity Hills. Our approach and techniques are meant to highlight what's special about the vineyard.

properties.title: Denison Cellars
properties.description:
Denison Cellars is the winemaking passion of Tim and Denise Wilson. Having lived in many different wine regions – from Washington state to the Central Coast of California – it was their love of pinot noir that brought them to the Willamette Valley of Oregon. 

Launched with the 2009 vintage, Denison Cellars truly is a boutique winery, as annual production averages only 1,000 cases. 

Winemaker Tim Wilson is committed to producing wines with a focus on purity of fruit while showcasing the attributes that make each vineyard unique. This commitment is accomplished through Tim’s identification of exceptional grapes sourced from small, sustainably-farmed vineyards within and throughout the Willamette Valley. Focus is placed on producing small lots of ultra-premium wines showcasing the Willamette Valley’s extraordinary appellations and vineyards. Denison Cellars is based in the heart of the Willamette Valley, producing wines in McMinnville and holding winemaker-led tastings at NW Cru, located at 325 NE Evans Street in McMinnville, every Saturday between 11 am and 5 pm. Tastings are open to the public; reservations not required.

properties.title: Division Winemaking Company
properties.description:
Our Portland based urban winery strives to make delicious, interesting & balanced-low intervention wines that are reflective of vintage & place.

We focus on the responsible stewardship of our terroir expressive vineyards that celebrate our favorite grape varieties, and the ever-expanding range and possibilities of Pacific Northwest winemaking.

properties.title: Dobbes Family Winery
properties.description:
Founded in 2002, Dobbes Family Winery/Wine by Joe embodies over three decades of winemaking. Our roots are solid and we are driven by an unrelenting commitment to consistently produce wines that exude the best of Oregon. We embrace new possibilities and try to deliver an evolved wine experience for everyone we interact with. Our group of dynamic people and partners take pride in every single aspect of our business and output. We stand proud and progressive, inclusive and intentional, at every turn. Our passion fuels us and we exude it in everything we do. We are proud to be rooted in tradition but unbound by convention.

We make wines that are true to their varietal character, showing as much depth, elegance, opulence and balance as possible. There’s always a new wine emerging from our winemaking team, fueled by their passion for continuous exploration of Oregon’s terroirs and endless wine possibilities. Our portfolio of wine includes: Chardonnay, Grenache Blanc, Viognier, Rosé of Pinot Noir, Single Vineyard Pinot Noir, Cuvée Pinot Noir, Syrah, Late Harvest Viognier, and Port-Style Syrah.

properties.title: Domaine Divio
properties.description:
At Domaine Divio, Bruno Corneaux applies his traditional Burgundian crafting skills to Pinot Noir and Chardonnay. Achieved through minimal intervention and sustainability in respect of the land, the wines emphasize the elegance and complexity of our Willamette Valley grapes. 

Visit our tasting room to discover the beautiful scenery of the Ribbon Ridge AVA and enjoy a unique experience of Oregon wines in a Burgundian style.

properties.title: Domaine Drouhin Oregon
properties.description:
Established in 1987, Domaine Drouhin Oregon is owned by the Drouhin Family of Burgundy. Fourth-generation winemaker Véronique Boss-Drouhin produces wines of distinction, balance, elegance, and longevity. The family’s principled approach to viticulture and winemaking and long experience with Pinot Noir and Chardonnay have earned an international reputation for excellence.

We are honored to invite you to experience wines made with French Soul from Oregon Soil. Our tasting room is open for indoor & outdoor tastings by appointment only from 10am to 3pm, daily. Please call us on (503)864-2700 with any questions.

properties.title: Domaine Lumineux
properties.description:
Established in 2019, Domaine Lumineux is a continuation of the Coppola family’s long-standing winemaking tradition. 

Previously known as Domaine de Broglie, Domaine Lumineux honors the profound interplay between light and wine. Its name pays homage to Francis Ford Coppola’s deep fascination with science, specifically the quantum theory of light and how its wave-particle duality offers a glimpse into the mysteries and intricacies of the universe. 

The Domaine Lumineux vineyard is located in the renowned Dundee Hills in the northern Willamette Valley, with a charming tasting room in the heart of historic downtown Newberg. 

Domaine Lumineux wines are made exclusively from fruit grown on our 42-acre, LIVE Certified sustainable Dundee Hills vineyard.

properties.title: Domaine Roy & Fils
properties.description:
Domaine Roy & Fils was founded in 2012. We craft exceptional wines through meticulous stewardship of the land, a timeless approach to winemaking, and a commitment to sustainable farming. Our wines exhibit uncompromising precision and character, allowing for a full expression of vintage and terroir.

We are open daily from 11 AM to 5 PM. Explore our tasting experiences and reserve your visit through TOCK. Walk-ins are also welcome.

We look forward to hosting you!

properties.title: Domaine Serene
properties.description:
Come experience wine country like never before, with a taste of Burgundy in the heart of the Willamette Valley. Founded in 1989 by Ken and Grace Evenstad, Domaine Serene set out to craft world-class Pinot Noir. After three decades, we are proud to share our award-winning sparkling wine, as well as Pinot Noir and Chardonnay from both our Oregon and Burgundy estate vineyards. Our tasting room offers a range of experiences for every guest. Whether you’re enjoying a glass or bottle of wine, or embarking on a more immersive journey, we have something for all. We have Sommelier-led flights that dive deep into our rich story, or opt for a self-guided tasting with a curated food pairing. Walk-ins are always welcome, or you can make a reservation for more expanded experiences. Whether you wish to relax or learn, Domaine Serene is your premier destination for creating unique, unforgettable moments.
 
To explore our experiences or book online, visit: www.domaineserene.com/visit-us
Or contact us at (503) 864-4600 or hospitality@domaineserene.com

properties.title: Domaine Willamette
properties.description: Domaine Willamette produces world-class méthode traditionnelle sparkling wines with an underground aging cellar at the Demeter® Certified Biodynamic Bernau Estate Vineyard in the Dundee Hills. The winery offers Oregon-inspired hospitality with wine and food pairings, stunning views, educational tours and beautiful gardens overlooking the Dundee Hills.

properties.title: Dominio IV
properties.description:
At Dominio IV, we indulge in the creative.
We make wines and experiences that we enjoy, and we hope to share these delights with you!
From our wide array of varietals to our copious amounts of cozy places to sit, we invite you to join us on our farm in Carlton. 

Come into the farmhouse and grab your first splash of Sauv Blanc, Viognier, bubbles, Chardonnay, or rosé, head out to the front porch and enjoy the vineyard view with a pinch of Pinot Noir, walk up to our labyrinth with a sip of Syrah or Grenache, and finish with a taste of Tempranillo in our historic barn. 

Join us for a tasting, glass, or bottle daily from 11am - 5pm. Appointments and walk-ins welcomed.

properties.title: Duck Pond Cellars and Ransom Spirits
properties.description: Duck Pond Cellars is an iconic winery with over 30 years of history, deeply rooted in the heart of Oregon’s Willamette Valley. Since opening our doors in 1993 in historic Dundee, we’ve made it our mission to provide a welcoming gathering place for both locals and visitors. The Willamette Valley is the heart and soul of what we do, influencing every aspect of our winemaking. Our award-winning winemaker, Julia Cattrall, ensures that no detail is too small. Each bottle is crafted with minimal intervention, delivering handcrafted consistency year after year.

properties.title: Dukes Family Vineyards
properties.description: Dukes Family Vineyards artisan wines are hand-crafted in a resurrected 1870's Historic Barn wine making facility. A minimalist approach to wine making is utilized allowing a true reflection of the unique terroir and complex characteristics of their Eola-Amity Hills AVA vineyard. All estate grown fruit, the wines are an elegant addition to your favorite meal and delightful to share with your friends and family. Dukes Family Vineyards is Pat and Jackie Dukes realization of a dream; a passion growing out of a love of great food and exceptional wine. As conservators of the land, they are committed to low-impact, ecologically sound sustainability in the farming methodologies and to the continual improvement of the vineyard environment. The vineyards of Dukes Family Vineyards are Sustainable and Salmon Safe certified by LIVE.

properties.title: Durant Vineyards
properties.description:
Experience Durant Vineyard’s estate-grown and crafted wines from our beautiful Dundee Hills Wine Tasting Room at Red Ridge Farms. Perched above the dramatic Willamette Valley, the Durant Vineyards Tasting Room features incredible views of Mt. Hood, Mt. Jefferson, and miles of diverse agricultural land. Enjoy seasonal bites from our talented Culinary Directors, expertly crafted to pair with our wines. Whether you’re a wine enthusiast or a casual taster, we provide a welcoming atmosphere to immerse yourself in the beauty of our surroundings and the quality of our wines. 

Reservations are recommended but not required. To make a reservation, please call 503.864.2000 Ext. 1 or visit www.durantoregon.com. We look forward to welcoming you!

properties.title: Dusky Goose
properties.description:
Experience Dusky Goose, where every wine reflects our unwavering commitment to quality. Sourced from a handful of the Willamette Valley’s most esteemed sites—including our estate Rambouillet Vineyard in the Dundee Hills—our wines capture both the purity of each vineyard and the expressive style that defines Dusky Goose. Winemaker Hans Van Dale continues the legacy established by founding winemaker Lynn Penner-Ash, crafting Pinot Noir and Chardonnay with texture, complexity, and age-worthiness.

Join us for a tasting designed for those who seek more than just a glimpse of Oregon Pinot Noir—our curated flights showcase the precision of our vineyard selections and the depth of our cellar.

We welcome both reservations and walk-ins daily from 11:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m.

For a deeper exploration, our Private Library Tasting offers a rare opportunity to experience older vintages in the exclusive setting of our Pendleton Room.

To book a same-day tasting, call 503-857-5776.

properties.title: Eila Wines
properties.description: We are a small winery based in Oregon. We seek to make exceptional, distinct, elegant wines from outstanding vineyards in the Willamette Valley.

properties.title: Élevée Winegrowers
properties.description: A Study in Willamette Valley Terroir. 'Single Vineyard' Pinot Noir and 'German Origin' Cool-Climate Whites that showcase the distinct and dramatic flavors of the Valley's diverse Appellations. Enjoy sweeping views of the Dundee Hills and Willamette Valley from our garden patio as you taste directly with Winegrowers/Owners, Tom & France and their amazing Bernese Mountain Dog, Four.  This is a micro-boutique, passion project from Burgundy Trained, UC Davis Educated Veteran Winegrower Tom Fitzpatrick.

properties.title: Elk Cove Vineyards
properties.description:
Named for the herd that roams the nearby hills, Elk Cove Vineyards was founded in 1974 and was the first vineyard and winery in what is now the Yamhill-Carlton AVA. Tucked into the foothills of the Coast Range Mountains, the tasting room has spectacular views of the surrounding vineyards.

Second-generation winemaker Adam Campbell produces excellent Single Vineyard Pinot Noirs as well as crisp aromatic Pinot Gris, Pinot Blanc and Riesling with an emphasis on estate-grown fruit. Living his whole life among the vines, Adam has tremendous respect for the land: "High-quality wine really starts in the vineyard. The time I spend stomping through our nearly 400 acres of vines not only helps us make good decisions in viticulture, it also informs our winemaking decisions."

properties.title: Et Fille Wines
properties.description:
Our Story is About Family

It is how we started, why we make wine, and what we hope accompanies our wines at your dinner table. Et Fille, which means "and daughter", was co-founded in 2003 by father and daughter winemakers and is now in its second generation with daughter Jessica making elegantly complex wine inspired by her late father's legacy and daughter's future. We are a Certified B Corp crafting wines from our sustainably farmed vineyards in Oregon's Willamette Valley.

properties.title: Evening Land Vineyards
properties.description: Evening Land Vineyards is anchored in the Seven Springs estate, a renowned vineyard in the Eola-Amity Hills, where ancient volcanic soils and the cool, forested landscape come together to shape the character of the land and create ideal conditions for growing Pinot Noir and Chardonnay that are pure, expressive, and a true reflection of this unique terroir. At Evening Land, every bottle tells the story of place.

properties.title: Evesham Wood Vineyard Winery
properties.description: The focus of our small winery is Pinot noir and Chardonnay produced from our 12 acre certified organic estate vineyard, Le Puits Sec, and other vineyards in the Eola Hills near Salem. The distinctive character of our wines is achieved by the sourcing of non-irrigated, sustainably grown fruit, and a natural non-interventionist" approach in the cellar.

properties.title: The Eyrie Vineyards
properties.description:
The Eyrie Vineyards was founded with one goal in mind: to establish Pinot noir and Chardonnay in the climate most suited for them outside of Burgundy and make wines of the highest caliber and expression. After a world-wide search that included New Zealand and Portugal, founder David Lett planted the Willamette Valley’s first Pinot noir, Chardonnay, and Pinot gris, as well as 4 other cool-climate varieties, in 1965. 
For the last 20 vintages, Eyrie’s legacy has been stewarded by Jason Lett and a small but stalwart team of permanent crew. In his time at Eyrie, Jason and co. have transitioned the estate to fully no-till viticulture and Certified Organic and developed a procedure for certifying library wines that is unmatched in the world of wine. Today’s releases are more diverse than in the past with 21 different cuvées. In addition, Eyrie annually releases a handful of cellar-certified library wines directly from its deep collection.

properties.title: Failla Wines
properties.description: Failla Wines began in 1998, born of the opportunity to make expressive wines from single vineyard locations across California's Western Sonoma Coast. Ehren, never one for slowing down, rapidly expanded the Failla stable from two wines in 1998 to over 30 wines two decades later. Our expansion into the Willamette Valley is indicative of Ehren's forward-thinking nature and desire to constantly challenge himself with excellent vineyards in different regions. In 2001, we sourced fruit from the famous Goldschmidt Vineyard. Logistics proved a challenge, and we only made one vintage. Many years and a few plane trips later, we were offered Pinot from the legendary Seven Springs Vineyard, and the Oregon bug officially bit again. In 2016, we made 6 wines from all over the Willamette Valley (including Gamay!), and in 2017 we expanded the lineup to include Chardonnay, Riesling, and Gruner Veltliner. Long-term, we plan to develop a vineyard at our 80-acre site in the Van Duzer Corridor AVA, located southwest of our current Tasting Room in the Eola-Amity Hills.

properties.title: Fairsing Vineyard
properties.description:
Experience the beauty and bounty of Fairsing Vineyard. 

Fairsing Vineyard grows and crafts beautifully-textured Pinot noir, Chardonnay, and Sparkling high among the hills of the Yamhill-Carlton AVA. 

Enjoy our estate wines and inspired hospitality paired with panoramic views extending from the Cascades to the Coastal Mountain Ranges.

• SEATED TASTINGS include a flight of current release estate wines with (optional) artisan cheese plates available.

• ELEVATED SEATED TASTINGS include an array of charcuterie, cheese, fruits, nuts, spreads, and small bites to enjoy with a flight of current release offerings. 

• PATIO SEATING includes a flight of current release estate wines guided by a Fairsing team member on our patio with panoramic Valley views.

• ADIRONDACK SEATING combines bottle service with majestic views of the Valley. Priority access reserved for Fairsing Wine Club members. Inclement weather affects availability. Plan accordingly (pack an extra layer). Re-scheduling may be required.

Contact our tasting room at 503.560.8266 with inquiries. 

Enjoy spacious exterior patios, cozy fireplaces, an expansive lawn, and a meditative labyrinth just a few steps from the tasting room. 

Nestled within a certified sustainable forest, Fairsing Vineyard draws upon the enduring farming and merchant traditions of the owner's Irish ancestors.

The overall design of the Fairsing tasting room and event space is reminiscent of ancient structures found in Ireland's countryside.

Established in 2005, Fairsing is distinguished by its ancient sedimentary soils, varied topography, and biodiversity. 

Fairsing Vineyard is a certified member of the Low Input Viticulture and Enology (LIVE) and Salmon Safe programs, the American Tree Farm System, and a founding member of the Willamette Valley Oak Accord.

Fairsing is planted to over 43 acres of Chardonnay and Pinot noir on south-facing slopes ranging from 400 to 750 feet in elevation. Selections include Dijon 76, 95, 667, 777, 114, 115, Pommard, Wadensvil, and Entav varieties.

With over 40 years of viticulture and winemaking knowledge, Robert Brittan ensures Fairsing wines reflect the abundant beauty and complexity of the estate.

properties.title: Five Fourteen Vineyards
properties.description: At Five Fourteen the combination of site and meticulous farming practices produce wines that are full of character and complexity. Join us for a truly special tasting experience of our estate grown wines. Open May-October or by appointment.

properties.title: Flâneur Wines
properties.description:
Since 2013, Flâneur Wines has crafted exceptional wines from their two estate vineyards in the Willamette Valley: La Belle Promenade (Chehalem Mountains AVA) and Flânerie Vineyard (Ribbon Ridge AVA). Winemaker Anthony Sereni creates an award-winning portfolio of Pinot Noir, Chardonnay, Gamay, Sparkling Wine and more, all organically dry farmed in-house. 

The name "Flâneur" captures the spirit of leisurely wanderers in 19th-century Paris, reflecting the idea that slowing down and embracing curiosity leads to a life of significance. This philosophy, inspired by Founder Marty Doerschlag, is embodied not only in the wines but also in the hospitality.

Flâneur Wines Tasting Room is housed in a century old Grain Elevator located downtown Carlton. Guests are met with warm hospitality, curated tastings, light bites and tours of the thoughtfully restored building. Once the heart of rural agriculture in the Willamette Valley is now the beating heart of fine wines, beautiful architecture and thoughtful hospitality.

properties.title: The Four Graces
properties.description:
As a leading Oregon winery, The Four Graces specializes in Pinot Noir as well as small production Pinot Gris and Pinot Blanc. Our wines come from four estate vineyards located in Willamette Valley’s Dundee Hills, Yamhill-Carlton & Van Duzer Corridor AVAs, as well as other top sites in the Willamette Valley. The Four Graces is committed to making site specific wines that are rich, complex and allow the fruit to be elegantly expressed.

Neighboring the Black Walnut Inn & Vineyard, The Four Graces hospitality center offers indoor and outdoor tasting experiences with stunning views of our estate vineyards. We feature curated tasting flights highlighted by seasonal food pairings and bites prepared daily by local chef Chase Williams. Choose our Sip & Savor experience that includes 5 wines paired with 5 exceptional bites; A Graceful Expedition is a 6-wine tasting flight that includes an amuse bouché; or the Anthology experience available exclusively to members of the Foley Food & Wine Society that includes thoughtfully chosen wines paired with a menu that is equal parts food and are traditionally inspired.

properties.title: Fullerton Wines
properties.description:
Fullerton Wines explores the rich and varied AVAs of Oregon by expressing the distinct personality of each vineyard and vintage in the bottle. Founded in the Fullerton family garage in 2011, the winery is a deeply personal family collaboration. Winemaker Alex Fullerton first got into the industry when his father Eric suggested the career path while wine tasting in the Willamette Valley after Alex had just graduated from the University of Oregon in 2010. That conversation led to Alex having a harvest internship with Penner-Ash Wine Cellars which started the very next day. When making their first vintage in 2011, Alex and Eric partnered with their good friend Roger Wallberg and made a few barrels of Pinot Noir. Despite the challenging vintage, the wine impressed and Fullerton Wines was born.

The Fullerton Wine Bar & Tasting Room in Portland is authentically Pacific Northwest with a Scandinavian heart. Guests come for hygge hospitality, wines that explore Oregon’s AVAs, and abundantly creative dishes.
You will experience the same Scandinavian heart in the tasting room at the winery in Corvallis that is open for tastings daily 11 am - 4 pm. The food will be limited to our well-known and appreciated Cheese & Charcuterie Platters. We look forward to hosting you and sharing our wines and our story with you.

A full menu during opening hours and monthly winemaker's pairing dinners with three distinct menu choices: vegan, Pescatarian, and Traditional.

Elegant and distinctive Pinot noir, Syrah, Chardonnay, Pinot Gris, and Viognier. We look forward to meeting you and your friends while sharing our beautiful and age-worthy wines.

Signature Flight: $30 per person, waived with a wine purchase of $200. One complimentary tasting or glass pour per month for wine club members and up to five guests. 

AVA Flight: $50 per person, waived with a wine purchase of $200. We produce a single vineyard designated Pinot Noir from each of the eleven AVAs (American Viticultural Area) of the Willamette Valley. The flight explores six different single vineyard Pinot noirs from six different AVAs. A knowledgeable team member will walk you through the different characteristics of each wine-growing region, as well as our winemaker, Alex Fullerton's winemaking style vintage characteristics.

Library Flight: $60 per person, waived with a wine purchase of $200. We've pulled some of our best Pinot noirs and Reserve Chardonnay from our library, including our inaugural 2012 Five FACES Pinot noir. A team member will walk you through the vintages and the wines as you sip through different vintages and different AVAs (American Viticultural Area). 

The tasting room at our winery in Corvallis is open daily between the hours of 11 a.m. and 4 p.m. Walk-ins are welcome. We encourage groups of 6 and larger to call 503-267-7432 to make a reservation so that we are able to give you the best experience.

properties.title: Gemini Vineyards
properties.description: Welcome to Gemini Vineyards, a family-farmed estate situated on Laurelwood soil at 500ft elevation in the Chehalem Mountains. Gemini Vineyards was established in 1995. Our commitment to regenerative and sustainable farming on our site ensures that our wines are produced to display the characters of the terroir and the love of land and wine. For those looking to experience an authentic vineyard experience while sipping exceptional wine from the Chehalem Mountains, you'll find a welcomed spot at our table, right among the vines in a cozy, covered gazebo. You might just be greeted by our resident flock of sheep and chickens as you experience an intimate wine tasting and vineyard tour. Whether new to wine, or an active wine enthusiast, Gemini is unpretentious, with the strong belief that wine is for everyone. We focus on producing expressive Pinot Noir, Chardonnay, Gamay Noir, Pinot Gris, and Pinot Noir Rose. To honor our 30 years of vines and wine, Gemini Vineyards will be opening a tasting room across from the estate in Sherwood in late 2025. Please join us.

properties.Owner3: DE HAYR LUKE & DE HAYR EMILY
DE HAYR LUKE & DE HAYR EMILY
DE HAYR LUKE & DE HAYR EMILY

properties.title: Ghost Hill Cellars
properties.description: Ghost Hill Cellars is a fifth-generation farm, vineyard estate, and unforgettable event destination, sustainably tended by the Bayliss-Bower family (and the ghost of a murdered gold miner) since 1906. Neighboring the vineyards of Ken Wright, Tony Soter, and other Willamette Valley luminaries in the Yamhill-Carlton AVA, the family’s highly prized soils produce Pinot Noir of exceptional finesse and longevity, crafted by legendary Oregon winemaker Eric Hamacher. With a spectacular new hospitality center to match the award-winning wines, Ghost Hill Cellars is a family treasure far more enduring than a prospector’s lucky break.

properties.title: Gran Moraine Winery
properties.description: Gran Moraine wines shine a light on the monologue of the Gran Moraine Vineyard, lifting its voice and vibrant energy through a signature style of elegance and restraint, with a fresh through-line of acidity. A style in many ways antithetical to the natural tendencies of the Yamhill-Carlton sub-AVA that has established the winery as not only a Pinot Noir, but a Chardonnay and sparkling wine leader in the Willamette Valley.

properties.title: Granville Wine Co.
properties.description:
Granville Wine Co. is a small, family-owned winery rooted in the heart of Oregon’s famed Dundee Hills AVA in the Willamette Valley, passionately led by second-generation growers Ayla & Jackson Holstein. Built on a deep connection to the land and a belief that great wine begins in the vineyard, Granville embodies the true spirit of terroir-driven winemaking.

At the center of their philosophy is hands-on stewardship — from tending their own Holstein Estate Vineyard to sourcing fruit from sustainably farmed sites across the Valley — with a focus on producing expressive and elegant wines that reflect the unique volcanic soils of the region.

What Makes Granville Special:

Intimate, Appointments-Only Tastings: Enjoy personalized experiences at the Dundee Hills tasting house, from classic flights to immersive vineyard tours designed to reveal their winemaking philosophy and estate wines.

Estate & Small Lot Wines: Specializing in Pinot Noir, Chardonnay, and select sparkling wines, crafted with care and a deep sense of place.

Terroir-Driven Wines: Wines showcase the volcanic Jory soils and meticulous farming practices, resulting in focused, elegant expressions of the Willamette Valley.

Family-Centered Vision: A true boutique winery where the winemaking and vineyard teams live and breathe the land they cultivate — creating wines with authenticity, balance, and a story in every bottle.

Granville Wine Co. is ideal for visitors who appreciate thoughtful, handcrafted wines and value a quiet, personal tasting experience paired with breathtaking views of Oregon’s celebrated wine country.

properties.title: Haakon Lenai Vineyard and Tasting Room
properties.description:
HAAKON/ LENAI [OCK•EN LA•NAY] IS OUR SHARED PURSUIT OF OREGON’S FINEST PINOT NOIR AND CHARDONNAY—INSPIRED BY OUR BEAUTIFUL CHILDREN, THE NAMESAKES OF THIS FAMILY WINERY. THE WINES FOR HAAKON/ LENAI WINERY ARE CRAFTED EXCLUSIVELY TO SHOWCASE OUR ESTATE VINEYARDS.

WITH 500 CASES LIMITED PRODUCTION OF PINOT NOIR AND CHARDONNAY THESE WINES CAN ONLY BE FOUND IN THE HAAKON / LENAI TASTING ROOM AND EXCLUSIVE RETAILERS.

CODY & MARQUE WRIGHT

properties.title: Hazelfern
properties.description:
Hazelfern started their wine craft in 2006 while making five gallons of Pinot Noir in a dimly lit Portland, Oregon basement on the former (circa 1895) site of Hazel Fern Farms. In 2014, a quarter-life crisis pushed Bryan and Laura Laing to pack up their basket press, two daughters, and a ping pong table for life on a wine farm in the Chehalem Mountains of Newberg, Oregon.

Today, Hazelfern remains a passionate husband and wife winemaking team focused on producing delicious wines and providing an exceptional and unique Oregon wine tasting experience in the Hazelfern wine barn.

Hazelfern wines are best paired with special good people, great stories, loud music, and Tuesday night farm dinners!

Hope to see you soon on the farm!

properties.title: Holocene Wines
